communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

In a letter dated August 27, 2018, the U.S. House of Representatives Committee on Energy and Commerce send a letter to MITRE regarding the CVE program [1]. In it, the house committee recommended that MITRE perform biennial reviews of the CVE program [2] to be submitted . I am requesting a copy of these reviews for 2018 or the first year they were submitted, as well as copies of the reports submitted in 2025. These were to be submitted to the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) and the managing agency of the CVE program, which is the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA).

[1] https://embed.documentcloud.org/documents/4788035-082718-MITRE-Recommendations-for-CVE-Program-1/
[2] https://www.cve.org/

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brian Martin

title: 2017 MITRE Response to US House of Representatives Committee on Energy and Commerce

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

ATTN: Committee on Energy and Commerce

I am requesting a copy of the response sent by MITRE, in response to the US House of Representatives Committee on Energy and Commerce letter to MITRE dated March 31, 2017. The Committee's letter is referenced below, and was signed by Greg Walden, Tim Murphy, Marsha Blackburn, and Robert E. Latta. The Committee letter requested a response from MITRE due no later than April 13, 2017.

Reference: https://web.archive.org/web/20180924011238/https://archives-energycommerce.house.gov/sites/republicans.energycommerce.house.gov/files/documents/033117%20MITRE%20CVE%20Letter.pdf

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brian Martin
